---
title: "statuncertainty"
output: github_document
---

# statuncertainty

## Statistical Uncertainty Quantification for Predictive Models

`statuncertainty` is an R package for estimating and visualizing uncertainty in binary classification performance metrics through bootstrap resampling.

The package implements non-parametric bootstrap methods for estimating confidence intervals around the area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (ROC-AUC).

## Motivation

Predictive models are frequently evaluated using point estimates of performance metrics. However, these estimates can be highly variable, especially in small biomedical and high-dimensional datasets.

This package provides tools for quantifying uncertainty in model evaluation by repeatedly resampling training data and estimating the distribution of model performance.

## Features

### Bootstrap AUC Confidence Intervals

The package provides:

- bootstrap estimation of ROC-AUC
- percentile-based confidence intervals
- bootstrap sampling distributions
- uncertainty visualization

### Visualization

Bootstrap distributions can be visualized to assess:

- variability in predictive performance
- confidence interval width
- model stability

## Installation

Install the development version from GitHub:

```r
# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("jen-h-lu/statuncertainty")
